author: Andrio
comment_content:
You learn how the internet works and how computers communicate with each other. The course covers the five Internet layer stack and teaches from the top to bottom (except the lowest one physical layer). There might be some extra contents such as wireless networking, security, etc. and you might also learn something about hacking.
About 90% concepts and 10% probabilities. Some stuff from COMP3711, 3511, and MATH2411 are also there. Four written assignments and one programming project. Then midterm and final.
Programming project was quite challenging. Basically you need to make a multi-threaded server-client using java from scratch (no skeleton).
comment_teaching: Teachings are good. The instructor actively asks for feedback during the lectures. The TA is also easily approachable.
comment_grading:
Written assignments (4): 20%
Programming assignment: 15%
Midterm: 25%
Final: 40%
Midterm exam format is strange as it feels like doing homework.
Finals are okay, some theories, some calculations, and some probabilities.
comment_workload:
Written assignments are usually theories, so you can look at the textbook or google it. Sometimes there are questions that need calculations so it might take time. Rarely probability questions come out. These ones are hard and take most of the time.
Programming assignment: you should really start early especially if you don't know java well. In my opinion, the hard part is more on the multi-threading than networking.

author: stereo
comment_content:
Internet Comm 有5個layer，成個course就係教唔同hosts (router, endpoint, DNS etc.) 之間點樣係呢5個layer溝通 (protocol)

所以，呢個course 你會學到好多protocol 同埋hosts 之間既 comm policy
Application, Transport, Network, Link Layer
Physical layer冇notes，所以得4個chapter
comment_teaching: 好少上堂💩  通常都同TA email多，TA 幾盡責，如果佢地改分可以快少少就更好
comment_grading:
總分median，below mean，B-，AB range加埋有一半，即係C range佔另外一半 lol

功課有google 就做到
Midterm below mean 幾分
Final冇出分，直接出grade :D

Project 要寫一個HTTP server，語言不拘 (不過lab code 用C，既然可以參考當然跟住用linux C...)
mean 90/120
comment_workload:
一課百幾版lecture notes + chapter summary瘋狂背完入去考，不過呢科得4課，內容比3511少，worload主要來自deadline fighter 背書

project 其中兩個計分項 (chunked encoding & gzip compression) 好似冇教過...? 靠google 做到chunked, gzip索性冇做
而且deadline 係exam period 中間，同時間溫書+做proj

author: Kokomi
title: OK
comment_content:
Nothing special. Just standard 5 layers of network. The content and slides mainly follows the book Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach.

The first half of the course (application layer & transport layer) is practical and somehow interesting. However, after the midterm, the content (network layer & link layer) seems to become more and more boring. As far as I'm concerned, The second half of the course seems not so useful unless you want a job that is highly related to (the bottom of) network.

p.s. The professor of this semester is the designer of COMP 2611, so it is not surprising that this course is like another 2611 to me.
comment_teaching:
Satisfactory teaching. The professor can explain things very clealy. He usually includes a lot of examples, drawings and simile to help us understand the contents better. Generally speaking, his teaching is slow-paced, so I think is not hard to keep up with him in lectures. Bad news is that he failed to finish everything in time, so he posted some extra videos for us to watch. He is also willing to answer questions.

Lab tutorials, however, are completely useless. The TAs just read everything on their slides, without any passion. I found reading their slides myself far more efficient than listening to the TAs. I guess their research area has nothing to do with computer network.

Recordings are available on canvas. Although the prof claimed that he will not release the recordings if few people go to his lecture, we ended up getting all of them.
comment_grading:
### Grading Scheme

- Homeworks: 20%
- Midterm: 25%
- Project: 20%
- Final: 35%

### Homeworks

3 homeworks in total. Each of them takes around 1-2 hours. Some of them may ask you to summarize a protocol taught in class, while some ask you to self-study certain new topic and give answers to some questions never mentioned in class. For me, it is very hard to get high marks, possibly because I am a lazy guy who don't like to write too many words for a question >_<

stat:
- Homework 1: Low=45.0 LQ=52.00 Mean=54.58 Median=56.00 UQ=56.00 High=60/60
- Homework 2: Low=43.5 LQ=49.13 Mean=51.99 Median=52.50 UQ=55.38 High=59/60
- Homework 3: Low=28.0 LQ=45.13 Mean=46.86 Median=47.75 UQ=50.00 High=56/60

### Project

The project is a chatting room with file sharing functionality. The coding is done in C. I spent ~2 days on it. Basically you need to use the things taught in labs to fill in the blanks of the skeleton code. I think this project is not harder than the one in COMP 2611, mainly because C is at least better than assembly ;)

They provided 5 test scripts for us to test our implementation, so it is not hard to get a high score. There is also one bonus task (2%), so you will see the highest score is 22 out of 20.

stat:
Low=4 LQ=19.6 Mean=18.82 Median=UQ=20 High=22/20

### Exams

Generally speaking, the questions in the exams are much trickier than the examples in lectures. For some questions, you need to be kind of smart and also some math capability to get to the point.

The questions come from various but reasonable sources. At the end of the midterm, I heard some student saying that they have seen a question in a past paper, although I don't know where they get the past papers because the prof never released any. The prof also claimed that a question in the midterm comes from the exercises in the textbook. Unfortunately, I didn't check the textbook and messed up the midterm.

As for the final, I remember that one question was very similar to an example in the extra videos. Another question comes from the addition notes at the end of some slides. Besides, the prof mentioned in class that some content will probably appear in the final, and it did. Therefore, I feel that you may need to pay attention to all those details in the course to do well in the exam.

By the way, you can print all the lecture slides and bring them to the exam. However, you cannot add additional marks or notes on them. Calculators are allowed but proved not useful.

stat:
- Midterm: Low=19 LQ=47 Mean=56.06 Median=54.25 UQ=67.88 High=85.5/100
- Final: Low=0 LQ=61.5 Mean=66.61 Median=67 UQ=77.5 High=90/100

### Others

Although attendance is not included in the grading scheme, the prof randomly take attendance in lectures and tutorials. You also need to submit some quiz after each lab session, which also does not appear in the scheme. I have no idea whether these things are included in grading or not.

### My Scores

- Homework 1: 45/60
- Homework 2: 51/60
- Homework 3: 47/60
- Project: 22/20 (with bonus)
- Midterm: 71.25/100
- Final: 90/100

Letter grade: A+

The grading is very good, I would say.
comment_workload: Please refer to the grading part for details. I think the workload is very low for a 4xxx-level course. You can treat the workload as another COMP 2611.

author: DankME
title: low workload
comment_content:
Networking is a must for CS students. Focus on UDP, TCP, IP, and Socket, they’re common in job interviews. The first half is engaging, covering protocols like HTTP, DNS, and P2P networks. The later part (post-midterm) feels like OS and is less interesting.

Course Syllabus
Introduction (3 lectures)

Introduction to Networking

Network Architectures

Application Layer (5 lectures)

Application Layer Protocols: HTTP, SMTP, DNS, Streaming

Peer-to-Peer Networks and Applications (BitTorrent)

Transport Layer (7 lectures)

The TCP/IP and Internet Architecture

Transport Protocols: TCP and UDP

Reliable data transfer
TCP Congestion Control

Network Layer: datapath and

Network Layer Control (7 lectures)

Network Protocol: IP

Internetworking

Switch/Router Architecture
Packet Forwarding/Scheduling and buffer management
Network Routing and Routing Algorithms

Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR)

IP Version 6 (IPv6)

Link Layer and Local Area Networks (3 lectures)

Link Layer Issues Error detection and Framing

Multiple Access Protocols: Aloha, CSMA

Ethernet

Bridges and Switches

Textbook
James F. Kurose and Keith W. Ross Computer Networks: A Top Down Approach Featuring Internet, 7th/8th Edition, Pearson,  (6th Edition also is fine but it lacks some small parts)

Reference Books
Larry L. Peterson and Bruce S. Davie, Computer Networks: A Systems Approach, Morgan Kaufmann Publishers

W. Richard Stevens, Bill Fenner and Andrew M. Rudoff, UNIX Network Programming Vol. 1, 3rd ed., Addison-Wesley.
comment_teaching: The professor explains concepts well, pay attention or you’ll get lost. Join the Facebook group for questions. He’s passionate about networking and approachable after class. He checks for plagiarism/AI in homework and patrols exams, banning notes with handwriting on printed slides.
comment_grading:
Grading
got A
Midterm: 25% (Scored 76.5/100, Mean: 56.06, Median: 54.25)
Final Exam: 35% (Scored 74/100, Mean: 66.61, Median: 67)
Homework (2-3): 20% (Scored ~mean)
Project: 20% (Scored 22/22, Mean: 18.82, Median: 20)
comment_workload: With AI tools, revising past papers is enough for prep. However, you must review lecture recordings, as some tested content isn’t on slides but mentioned in class. Expect 2-3 homeworks and a project, which are manageable with AI.

author: 吃花椒的驴
title: 龟极好
comment_content: 从上（应用层）到下（数据链路层）讲解整个网络的结构
comment_teaching: 问题来了，我没怎么去听课😂
comment_grading:
极好

作业（×3）：20%
PA：20% 正常分 + 2% Bonus 分 = 22%
期中考试：25%
期末考试：35%

以下各部分使用“满分, [Low, LQ, Mean, Median, UQ, High], 个人得分”的格式来写
作业 1：60, [45, 52, 54.58, 56, 56, 60], 48
作业 2：60, [43.5, 49.13, 51.99, 52.5, 55.38, 59], 49
作业 3：60, [28, 45.13, 46.86, 47.75, 50, 56], 49.5
PA：22, [4, 19.6, 18.82, 20, 20, 22], 20
期中考试：100, [19, 47, 56.06, 54.25, 67.88, 85.5], 85.5
期末考试：100, [0, 61.5, 66.61, 67, 77.2, 90], 66.5
本人最终成绩：A-

可以看出来，除了期中考试以外，我其余部分的成绩都不怎么好，这样还能拿到 A-range 真的很意外，我原本预估 B-range 的

期末考试里有一题的格式老师只在课上说了该怎么写，课件里没说，导致我一开始这题就是 0 分，去申诉申诉回来 5 分
comment_workload: 很少，就几个作业一个 PA

author: CS水泡
comment_content:
Network has 5 layers, everything from application to the physical wires. If you have taken ELEC1200, this is very similar.

Nothing new, just brings you to appreciate how the internet is basically held together by duct tape and still functions.
comment_teaching:
Lectures are fine, but the TA did not care whether we live or die.

There are a total of 4 labs which is not enough to finish the programming assignment, nor does it relate to the content taught in class. The programming assignment is basically to teach yourself barebone socket programming in C.

Teaching in class is useful as professor reveals a lot on what he will put in the exams. I asked him to go faster when he tried to teach distance vector for the second time and he insisted that was important.
comment_grading:
4 Written Assignments (20% total)
1 Programming Assignment (15%)
Midterm (25%)
Final (40%)

Mean (77% overall) is anywhere from C to B-, 10% above mean is A.
comment_workload:
Not much workload outside of the exams, since the written and programming assignments are very similar to previous years.

Attend the class if you want good grades. You can even ask the professor how to do the written assignment question. He won't dodge your question since the assignments were exclusively handled by the TA.

author: kverma
comment_content:
Mostly theoretical. Some concepts watered down for better understanding. Practical applications not apparent so it doesn't seem that interesting.

Syllabus
Principles of computer network architectures and communication protocols

The OSI reference model

Switching and multiplexing techniques

Data link, network, transport and application layers

LAN and medium access protocols

Network programming
comment_teaching: The prof explains content well. She makes an effort to go deep yet keep stuff digestible for students. She also discusses relevant research if time allows, which  makes things interesting.
comment_grading:
Our batch had 22 people so grading wasn't that harsh.
Midterm Exam,  30%
Final Exam,  35%
Homework,  21%
Project,  14%

88.5 overall -> A
comment_workload:
Light. 3 assignments, one project,
Assignments are theory based - kinda like those in 3511 os - each takes 1-2 hrs
Project is making a mail server using Java. Skeleton code is given so the task takes 5 hours max.
Course also has midterm and final
Final is not cumulative - consists of content covered after midterm.
Both exams aren't crazy difficult but reading the textbook for concepts u don't understand is advised.

author: JS
comment_content:
Layers: app, trans, network/internet, link
4621 dicussed all layers, which involves simple calculcation. Student who get C+ in MATH1014 should find no difficuties in understanding the logic.

algorithm: dijkstra, bellman ford
These are the algorithm used in discussing routing algorithms, which are easy as they are just steps to follow. No variety.

Most of the content is concrete and you just have to memorise them. This course mainly discussed on HOW the internet protocol is enforced rather than WHY.

But the content is rather insufficient in my opinion.
comment_teaching:
Kai is acceptable. The pace is slow but not in depth.
The tutorial is less then enough. TAs are not bad and in fact they are good at teaching concepts.
comment_grading:
Since most of the students got 7X and higher, the A range is estimable around 95~100 as I get very close to this score and got a B+.
So if you are not really familiar with networking or bad at it, the grade will not be proportional to your effort you paid for this course.
In short, not good grade.
comment_workload:
4~5(I forgot)HW. 1Ass. 1MT. 1Final.

HWs are mix of 70% SQ and 30% LQ.
PJ requires you to write an HTTP Server to serve HTML, images, pdfs, pptxs, css files. 20% bonus for gzip and chunk transfer. You can choose any language you like. I chose Java. Btw, the assignment requires you not to use 3rd party lib, just ordinary socket programming and construct the response message on your own.

MT and Final are similar to HW. Make sure you do all the HW.

Tutorials are about the assignment. They help.

Most of the student got ~80.

author: Zoellick Han
title: Nice Grade
comment_content: The course covers the classic seven-layer OSI model from the physical layer all the way up to the application layer (though these days people more often discuss things in terms of the TCP/IP four-layer model). It goes into fairly detailed explanations of the data link layer (things like Ethernet and ARP), the network layer (IP protocol along with routing protocols such as RIP, OSPF, and BGP), the transport layer (with a strong focus on TCP and UDP, especially TCP’s congestion control mechanisms), and several typical application-layer protocols (HTTP, DNS, SMTP, and P2P systems like BitTorrent). Occasionally it also touches on some more advanced topics such as Quality of Service (QoS), multicast, and IPv6. Overall, the course systematically walks you through the entire journey of how data packets travel from your device to the destination, helping you really understand how the different layers of the Internet work together underneath the surface.
comment_teaching: He is a very enthusiastic teacher who is also deeply familiar with the subject matter. I think he is one of the rare truly good professors at HKUST. If you're willing to chat with him about the course content after class, he is more than happy to spend a lot of time discussing things with you.
comment_grading: Compared to other COMP courses at HKUST, COMP4621 is noticeably more lenient when it comes to grading and final marks. The exams, however, are quite unique each time—the professor personally designs all the questions, so you can’t rely too heavily on memorizing past papers from previous years. For review, the best preparation is to thoroughly work through the end-of-chapter exercises in the textbook, as those problems essentially serve as the main question bank for both the midterm and final exams.
comment_workload: The workload for this course is very light. The assignments each take only about 2 hours, and there are just three of them throughout the entire semester. There’s also one project that lasts a month, but you can realistically finish it in 2–3 days.

author: namreh
comment_content:
As there is no reviews for the prof, here is a short summary.

This is COMP5621/CSIT5610 but with bad grading, as the course is very competitive.
All lectures are very knowledge-based. There's no hands-on exercise in the course.

Content
- Applicaiton Layer
- Transport Layer
- Network Layer
- Data Link Layer
comment_teaching:
He is passionate about teaching, but his teaching is very fast-paced. He wants to squeeze as much content as possible in the course.

He expects us to be good at Math. Have fun with all the derive formula in your assignments and exams.
comment_grading:
He teaches the simplest example in lectures, and gives incredibly difficult/tricky questions for you in exams.

If you are hard-working students who want to study harder for a better grade, unfortunately there is no exercises for you to practice.

Exams questions are very similar to COMP5621/CSIT5610. If you have source, A range is pretty much guaranteed. If not, then good luck.
comment_workload:
Workload is low.
2 written assignments, 1 programming assignment and 2 exams.

Some questions in the written assignments are designed by the prof himself and you probably cannot find the answers online.

Programming assignment is easy as long as you are willing to spend time doing it.
